diagrams.title: Access Specifiers Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Three nested boxes showing public, protected, and private access levels from most open to most restricted
elements: protected (middle box - accessible to class and derived)
private (innermost box - accessible only to class)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the hierarchy of access control in C++ classes

diagrams.title: Encapsulation Concept
diagrams.description: A capsule diagram showing data members inside, methods on the surface, and interface arrows connecting to external code
diagrams.purpose: Understand how encapsulation bundles data and provides controlled access

diagrams.title: Friendship in C++
diagrams.description: Two class boxes connected by a dashed line representing friendship, showing how friend class/function accesses private members
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how friendship allows cross-class access to private members
